rubrics: Facts should describe the one-shot prisoner's dilemma structure: two players, simultaneous choice, cooperate/defect.
Facts should capture the payoff ordering: temptation > mutual cooperation > mutual defection > sucker's payoff.
Facts should identify the dominant strategy logic and Nash equilibrium.
Facts should not drift into evolutionary, repeated, or population-level variants.
Exactly 2 players — the two prisoners. No referees, observers, or abstract agents.
Each player should have a has-objective relationship to at least one objective.
Objectives should be specific: minimize sentence, maximize personal freedom — not generic 'maximize utility'.
Player entities must include playerType (should be 'primary') and knowledge array.
Exactly 1 game entity with gameType 'prisoners-dilemma' and timing 'simultaneous'.
Strategies should be cooperate and defect for each player — 2 per player, 4 total max.
Strategy feasibility should be 'actual' for cooperate/defect, not 'dominated' (even if dominated in solution).
In a full pipeline run, plays-in and has-strategy relationships should link players to games and strategies.
Textbook games have no interaction history. Phase should produce zero entities and call complete_phase immediately.
Must include rationality assumption (players maximize payoff) — classification: 'game-theoretic'.
Must include one-shot assumption (no repeated interaction) — classification: 'game-theoretic'.
Must include simultaneous move assumption (no observing other's choice) — classification: 'structural'.
Should NOT list game rules (payoff structure) as assumptions — those are definitional.
Each assumption must have non-empty dependencies array referencing prior-phase entity IDs.
Must include mutual defection as the equilibrium-driven baseline scenario with highest probability.
Scenario probabilities must sum to 95-105%.
Each scenario must specify prediction_basis: 'equilibrium' for the Nash outcome.
Central thesis should state that mutual defection is the predicted outcome despite mutual cooperation being Pareto-superior.
Central thesis must include falsification_conditions.

priorContext.player-identification: [{"id":null,"ref":"fact-1","confidence":"high","rationale":"This establishes the fundamental setup and constraints of the prisoner's dilemma game","type":"fact","phase":"situational-grounding","data":{"type":"fact","date":"classical game theory","source":"game theory literature","content":"Two players are arrested and held separately, unable to communicate, and each must independently choose to either cooperate (stay silent) or defect (confess/betray)","category":"rule"}},{"id":null,"ref":"fact-2","confidence":"high","rationale":"The payoff structure is what creates the strategic tension and defines the dilemma","type":"fact","phase":"situational-grounding","data":{"type":"fact","date":"classical game theory","source":"game theory literature","content":"The payoff structure follows: mutual cooperation yields moderate sentences for both, mutual defection yields harsh sentences for both, and unilateral defection yields freedom for the defector while the cooperator receives the worst sentence","category":"rule"}},{"id":null,"ref":"fact-3","confidence":"high","rationale":"This captures the strategic logic that drives players toward the suboptimal outcome","type":"fact","phase":"situational-grounding","data":{"type":"fact","date":"classical game theory","source":"game theory literature","content":"Defection strictly dominates cooperation for each player individually - regardless of what the other player does, each player is better off defecting","category":"rule"}},{"id":null,"ref":"fact-4","confidence":"high","rationale":"This identifies the solution concept and highlights the core dilemma of individual vs collective rationality","type":"fact","phase":"situational-grounding","data":{"type":"fact","date":"classical game theory","source":"game theory literature","content":"The unique Nash equilibrium is mutual defection, even though mutual cooperation would yield better outcomes for both players","category":"rule"}}]
